desc: Durres is the practical, lower-frills side of the Albanian coast: a big Roman amphitheatre, a broad urban beach strip, evening xhiro along the seafront and an easy hop from Tirana's airport. It is more useful than pretty, but that makes it a workable base for budget beach time, Albanian city access and travellers who do not need a polished old-town resort.

todo.name: Durres Amphitheatre
todo.desc: Start with the Roman amphitheatre and nearby archaeological museum for the city's ancient layer.

todo.name: Durres beach promenade
todo.desc: Use the long urban beach for low-effort swims and evening walks rather than remote-coast solitude.

todo.name: Venetian Tower area
todo.desc: Link the tower, city walls and central squares in a short heritage walk.

todo.name: Tirana day trip
todo.desc: Use the short road link to add Albania's capital without changing base.

todo.name: Forum and bath remains
todo.desc: Add the scattered Roman and Byzantine fragments around the centre so the city reads as more than just beach frontage.

todo.name: Vollga promenade at dusk
todo.desc: Join the evening walk when locals come out and the seafront feels busiest and most social.

wifi.note: Generally good in seafront hotels, apartments and central cafes, but some older buildings and beach sections are less consistent once you move away from the main strip.

prac.fltNote: Durres is reached through Tirana International Airport rather than by flying into the destination itself. Tirana currently has a London Gatwick nonstop, so airport access is easy, but the last leg can still drag on in summer because beach traffic and Friday-Sunday movements slow the otherwise short transfer. Destination-level nonstop flags stay false because the flight is to Tirana, not Durres.
prac.lang: Albanian; English is common in tourism pockets
prac.visa: British citizens can visit Albania visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period, and GOV.UK says your passport should be valid for at least 3 months after the day you plan to leave.
alerts: Urban beach quality varies by section; choose accommodation carefully.
Weekend traffic from Tirana is heavy in summer.
Construction and beach-club noise can affect seafront stays.
Some of the best-known archaeology is compact rather than grand, so expectations should match the city's practical feel.

sweet: June and September are usually the best balance for warm sea weather, lighter traffic and a more relaxed seafront than peak July and August.
